prompt: Refactor this ARO code to use the qualifier-as-name form: `Compute the length from the message.`
actual:
The original statement uses the `Compute` action to calculate the length of a message. The qualifier-as-name form uses the qualifier `message` as the name in the feature set. The result is a more readable and idiomatic ARO feature set.

```aro
(Compute: Message Length) {
    Extract the <message> from the <request: body>.
    Compute the <length> from the <message>.
    Return an<OK: status> with the <length>.
}
```
expected: `Compute the <len: length> from the <message>.` — the qualifier names the operation, and `<len>` names the result so you can have multiple lengths in the same scope.
judge_reason: code uses <request: …> / <pathParameters: …> when the prompt never asked about HTTP
